gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (radeonsi, navi22, LLVM 19.1.7, DRM 3.59, 6.13.0-1-MANJARO)
notes:
I have been working with this since the beginning of the second open beta. I've verified it runs on my Windows partition, but will not launch properly in Linux, so it probably is not a hardware problem. When I start the game, the screen rapidly flashes between the game itself, and whatever was open behind it (or my desktop). Sounds come through (just like my headaches, with this going on) and once it gets through shaders and takes me to the title menu, the top menu can be clicked through, but items in the options menu cannot be clicked, selected with keyboard, or selected with controller. If I let the game continue running, it will crash my window manager and sometimes even kick me back to my login screen.

I've tested this with the default Proton, Proton Hotfix, Proton Experimental, and Proton Experimental bleeding-edge, the behavior is consistent across each. I have also tried with Wayland and with X11 (and in X11, with compositing on and off), and the behavior persists. My desktop environment is KDE6 and seems to work for every other game I have, including Monster Hunter World, so I don't think that's likely to be interfering with the game. I don't think there's any other way to get it running with functional menus and no flashing. I really wish I could play this game but I certainly won't be preordering it in this state.

notes:
This same setup ran both of the Open Beta Tests and the Benchmark Tool at a decent performance, with occasional crashing in gameplay for both. I have tried Proton 9.0-4, Experimental, Hotfix, and GE. The game crashes before getting to the optimizing screen. I think even the crash reporter is crashing, as it closes before the loading bar goes all the way. Tried launch options from other ProtonDB users, none fixed the issue. 

To make matters worse, Denuvo has locked me out for 24 hours TWICE, first when changing proton versions, and now when just changing launch options. Now I can't even try to troubleshoot the software I paid good money for until tomorrow.

I think I'm just going to have to wait for a patch from Capcom or Proton. I'm tired.

notes:
It seems that the update to driver version 580.76.05 has fully broken shader compilation. The The game crashes the same frame that any 3D graphics are rendered, despite taking 20-30 minutes to compile shaders each attempt.

Additionally the shader.cache2 file that was previously being created and used no longer appears in the game directory, and thus forces shader compilation on each boot.

I've tried a pleathora of Proton versions, before giving up and going back to latest GE.
